title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know
As we start the new year, we look into some of the factors that may play a role in determining freight rates for the year ahead. Read More

Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
With the global newsflow still subdued in the past week, following the Christmas and New Year holidays in large parts of the world, the macro picture was dominated by mounting concerns over the health of the global economy. Read More

Latest Trends
The IMO's carbon intensity regulations, specifically the carbon intensity indicator (CII) in place since 1 January, are likely to dramatically impact the LNG fleet supply, which could lead to ‘stranded' assets later this decade.

children.title: The year ended with critical changes

children.summary: The year ended with critical changes in grain flows due to geopolitical tensions between Russia and Ukraine.

children.title: Commodity markets were mixed
children.summary: Commodity markets were mixed as optimism over China's reopening was offset by concerns of further monetary tightening by central banks.

children.title: Demand recovery, however, is far from imminent
section_path: Demand recovery, however, is far from imminent
children.section_path_text: Demand recovery, however, is far from imminent
children.summary: China's recent allocation of oil quotas signals Beijing's intention to accelerate economic recovery whilst sticking to its longer-term goal of controlling emissions.

children.title: Fundamentals suggest that supply/demand balance is likely to remain tight this year

children.section_path_text: Fundamentals suggest that supply/demand balance is likely to remain tight this year
children.summary: Whilst 2022 ended with a fanfare, there is now a quieter feel for tanker markets as we step into 2023.

children.title: Consumers in China are still not actually buying more goods compared to a year ago
section_path: Consumers in China are still not actually buying more goods compared to a year ago
children.section_path_text: Consumers in China are still not actually buying more goods compared to a year ago
children.summary: As we have been stressing in our Weekly China Reports, inflation exceeding retail sales growth in both September and October in China was a troubling and almost unprecedented event.
